title: Israel Bombs Beirut Outskirts as Fighting With Hezbollah Escalates
summary: U.S. efforts for a truce in Lebanon appear to have stalled. Israel accused Hezbollah of firing at Israeli territory. Iran swiftly retaliated.

title: Iranians Sink Into Despair Facing War Deaths and Skyrocketing Inflation
summary: An imploding economy is causing hopelessness among both pro- and anti-government Iranians. And for those who wished for regime change, the letdown is palpable.

title: Oil Surges, Asian Stocks Sink After Iran Strikes Israel
summary: Oil prices jumped and stocks plunged after Iran fired missiles at Israel, raising doubts about the future of fragile cease-fire in the Middle East.

summary: Nithya Raman pulled into second place in the race to see who will face Mayor Karen Bass in November. There are more votes to be counted.

summary: Mr. Pelley, who was at CBS News for 37 years, including as a White House correspondent and a “60 Minutes” correspondent, spoke in his first extended interview since he was fired.

summary: The tech journalist and filmmaker was a surprise choice to lead a famously combative newsroom. His first week was chaos.

title: RFK Jr. Appears Disengaged on Many Health Department Matters Beyond Vaccines
summary: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has demonstrated little interest in managing his sprawling department as he focuses on food and vaccine policies, according to colleagues.

summary: His five-volume “Children of Crisis” series, published between 1967 and 1977, drew on his conversations with American children whose voices were not often heard.
